howItWorks.intro: This baby growth percentile calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Based on WHO/CDC growth chart data
Percentile shows what % of babies weigh less than yours
Consistent growth along any percentile curve is healthy
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is a normal baby weight percentile?
faq.answer: Any percentile between 5th and 95th is considered normal. What matters most is consistent growth along the same curve over time, not the specific percentile. A baby consistently at the 20th percentile is healthy.

faq.question: Should I worry about a low percentile?
faq.answer: Not necessarily. Genetics play a big role — small parents tend to have small babies. Concerns arise when a baby drops more than 2 percentile lines rapidly, or falls below the 3rd percentile. Talk to your pediatrician about any concerns.

faq.question: How often should I weigh my baby?
faq.answer: At regular checkups: 2 weeks, 1, 2, 4, 6, 9, 12, 15, 18, 24 months. Avoid daily weighing — normal fluctuations cause unnecessary worry. Weekly weights are useful for breastfed newborns in the first month if there are feeding concerns.
